---
name: Pattern variation
about: Propose a change to the chain, modes, or agent design
title: "[PATTERN] "
labels: pattern-variation, discussion
---

## What change you propose

<!-- Describe the variation. Be specific about what is being added, removed, or restructured. -->

## What problem this solves

<!-- What use-case does this address that the current pattern handles poorly or not at all? -->

## Which design principle this touches

<!-- The pattern preserves five principles. Indicate which ones your proposal would affect:

  1. Adaptivity in a single dedicated meta-agent
  2. Sequential composition for compounding insight
  3. Append-only shared state
  4. Bounded recursion
  5. Honest filters (triage can say "no")

  If your proposal weakens any of these, explain why the trade-off is worth it. -->

## What you considered and rejected

<!-- The pattern's design history is preserved in docs/anti-patterns.md (six rejected approaches).
     Did you consider variations of your idea that you decided against? List them and the reasoning.
     This mirrors the pattern's own design discipline. -->

## How this would be tested

<!-- A pattern change without a test plan is hard to evaluate. How would you (or a maintainer)
     validate that this variation produces better output than the current pattern? -->

## References

<!-- Links to similar patterns elsewhere, papers, blog posts, or prior discussions. -->
